zotero.abstractNote: We propose a new attack for ReLU networks which does not rely on gradients. Instead, it exploits the fact that ReLU networks are piecewise linear functions. We show that the decision boundary of a ReLU network is composed of hyperplanes and that the network is linear within each region defined by these hyperplanes. Our attack, which we call the Linear Region Attack, finds a point in a different linear region that is closer to the original input than any point found by gradient-based attacks. We demonstrate that our attack is effective against state-of-the-art defenses and that it can be used to certify the robustness of ReLU networks.

extra.Publication Title: Pattern Recognition: 40th German Conference, GCPR 2018, Stuttgart, Germany …, 2019
